chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ أَوْصَى لِقَرَابَاتِهِ وَ مَوَالِيهِ كَيْفَ يُقْسَمُ بَيْنَهُمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – The man who bequeaths to his relatives and his friends, how it should be distributed between them

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/77)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/77)
text: رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ فِي أَعْمَامِهِ وَ أَخْوَالِهِ فَقَالَ لِأَعْمَامِهِ الثُّلُثَانِ وَ لِأَخْوَالِهِ الثُّلُثُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, both together from Ibn Mahboub, from Ibn Raib, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who bequeathed with a third of his wealth among his paternal uncles and his maternal uncles. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘For the paternal uncles is two thirds, and for the maternal uncles is a third’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father all from ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab from Zurarah who has narrated the following: “This is about the case of a man who bequests one-third of his legacy to his paternal and maternal uncles. Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The paternal uncle receive two-thirds and the maternal uncle receives one-third.’”
